stories.headline: Xi Warns Trump on Taiwan After Warm Start to Summit
stories.summary:
US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping met in China for a closely watched summit.

It was a cordial start that later followed blunt remarks from Xi on the Taiwan issue. Annmarie Hordern reports from Beijing. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Hungary's Orbán-era wartime state of emergency ended, PM Péter Magyar says
stories.summary: The emergency governance framework was first introduced by the government of previous Prime Minister Viktor Orbán in 2020 as part of measures in response to the COVID-19 pandemic.

stories.headline: Latvian Prime Minister Evika Siliņa announces shock resignation after week of scandals
stories.summary: Latvian Prime Minister Evika Siliņa announced on Thursday she will resign, triggering the collapse of the country's governing coalition ahead of key October parliamentary elections. On top of this, two senior Latvian politicians have been detained during a corruption investigation.

stories.headline: Watch: Silicon Valley in Beijing—should Europe be worried?
stories.summary: Donald Trump has landed in Beijing for a summit that feels less like diplomacy and more like a Silicon Valley board meeting. And he hasn’t just brought officials; he has arrived with an "AI Cabinet" to navigate a high-stakes tech cold war.

stories.headline: Latvian PM resigns after row over stray Ukrainian drones
stories.summary: Drones bound for Russia crashed down in Latvia last week, prompting a political fallout.

stories.headline: 'They shot my neighbour in the head' - the lakeside city traumatised by war
stories.summary: Rebel fighters and Rwandan troops are accused of committing atrocities after capturing the DR Congo city of Uvira in December.

stories.headline: Britain’s Political Crisis Spooks Crucial Overseas Bond Buyers
stories.summary: Bond-market volatility sparked by the UK’s latest political crisis is serving up a reminder that the government can’t take foreign demand for granted.

stories.headline: Global Fund Managers Playing Catch-Up May Fuel S&P’s Next Leg Up
stories.summary: While US stocks have been going from one record to the next, global asset allocators haven’t quite kept up. And that may keep the market’s upward momentum intact as fund managers try to catch up.

stories.headline: Emerging-Market Stocks Rise as Alibaba, TSMC Join AI Hot Streak
stories.summary: Emerging-market stocks advanced for the third day this week as technology companies in Taiwan and China joined a hot streak by South Korean peers, buoyed by optimism that talks between the US and China will improve trade ties and boost demand for artificial intelligence-related hardware and services.

stories.headline: Brookfield Merges Insurance Unit as Flatt’s Overhaul Takes Hold
stories.summary: Brookfield Corp. is merging its shares with those of its insurance business as the firm pushes ahead with plans to transform itself into an investment-led insurer.

stories.headline: US Premarket Movers for May 14, 2026
stories.summary: S&P 500 Index futures rise 0.3% as of 7:47 a.m. in New York. Equities look set to extend Wednesday’s records with tech bulls egged on by Cisco’s premarket surge and the huge appetite shown for an AI chip IPO.

stories.headline: China Credit Growth Misses Expectations as New Loans Shrink
stories.summary: China’s credit expansion slowed far more than expected from a year earlier in April while banks extended less new loans, underscoring unusually anemic borrowing demand even for a typically slow month for lending.

stories.headline: Europe Is Turning Off Solar Just as Gas Market Tightens
stories.summary: Vast amounts of electricity are going to waste because grids can’t cope.

stories.headline: Two India-Bound LPG Tankers Add to Uptick in Hormuz Transits
stories.summary: Two India-bound vessels carrying cooking fuel from the Persian Gulf have transited the Strait of Hormuz, taking the number of large ships carrying oil, fuel and gas that have made it through the waterway since Sunday to ten.

stories.headline: A PE Firm’s Big Bet on Chemicals Falters in Historic Downturn
stories.summary: A $10 billion private equity firm that made its name as a chemicals specialist has seen a wave of its holdings fall into distress amid a severe industry downturn, pushing the investor to adapt its strategy.

stories.headline: OPEC+ Has Plan to Complete Series of Quota Hikes, Delegates Say
stories.summary: Key OPEC+ members aim to continue a series of oil quota increases over the next few months, completing the return of a layer of halted production — if only on paper — by the end of September, delegates said.

stories.headline: Retirement Funds Should Take Private Credit, Franklin CEO Says
stories.summary: Franklin Templeton Chief Executive Officer Jenny Johnson says retirement accounts are the “best place” for private credit and equity, given the illiquidity of such investments. “You can't get your money for 10 years, so you really need to be able to withstand the illiquidity,” Johnson says on Bloomberg Television. “On the other hand, a 1% additional return can mean 20% more just over a 20-year period in your retirement.” (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: The Debt Binge That Has Alphabet Execs Working the Night Shift
stories.summary: Google is hawking bonds in dollars, euros, pounds, francs and yen.

stories.headline: Climbers clear path to Everest summit past giant chunk of ice

stories.headline: Passengers allowed to leave norovirus-hit cruise ship
stories.summary: Passengers on the ship showing no symptoms are allowed to leave, authorities say, after 49 people fell ill from gastrointestinal sickness.

stories.headline: Tech Rally Rolls On as Cisco Highlights AI Fervor: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: The rally in technology stocks remained intact as Cisco Systems Inc.’s earnings and a hot streak in Nvidia Corp. highlighted the momentum around artificial intelligence.

stories.headline: India Bans Sugar Exports for Four Months to Protect Supply
stories.summary: India, the world’s second-largest sugar producer, has banned exports for more than four months, according to an official notice, as the government seeks to protect local supplies.

stories.headline: House Approves Year-Round Sale of Higher-Ethanol Gas
stories.summary: In a rare bipartisan vote, the House passed a bill allowing for the sale of an ethanol blend known as E15, over the objections of Republican hard-liners and some oil refiners.

stories.summary: Andriy Yermak was named by Ukraine's two anti-corruption agencies as a suspect in a money-laundering scheme.

stories.headline: Lebanon says two paramedics among 13 killed in Israeli strikes

stories.headline: Trump ally Kari Lake tapped to be US ambassador to Jamaica
stories.summary: Lake thanked President Trump for nominating her, saying on social media that Jamaica "is a country I know very well, full of incredible people".
